baseline.reason: Baseline claims the root cause is bfd_zalloc returning NULL (allocation failure) making var NULL at dereference, but line 745-746 already checks if(var==NULL) goto fail, so execution cannot reach line 864 via that path. The real bug is spec_var->file being NULL due to bfd_zalloc zero-initialization, not allocation failure.

vastdb.reason: Correctly identifies that bfd_zalloc zero-initializes struct fields (setting file to NULL), causing strdup(spec_var->file) at line 864 to crash. The fix (good.c:863 adding '&& spec_var->file != NULL') confirms this. Minor demerit for including tangential trigger-path steps (find_abstract_instance) not essential to the variable case.
